task: chart-stops-as-default — demote premium stop to a wide catastrophe cap; chart/ribbon/profit-lock primary

ship_config.label: both premium stops widened to -50% catastrophe cap (chart-stop primary)
ship_config.rationale: blueprint + C2/C3 + missed_week: fixed-% premium stops whipsaw 0DTE options out of eventual winners. At a wide -50% cap the premium stop becomes a catastrophe backstop only; chart-level stop + ribbon-flip-back + profit-lock chandelier + time-stop are the primary invalidation.

method: single-variable A/B, real OPRA option fills (use_real_fills=True), via autoresearch.runner.run_with_params (the params-driven path that reaches the engine — NOT raw run.py, which uses orchestrator defaults). Anchor scored via j_edge_tracker.score_candidate. DSR/PSR via lib.validation.gate.evaluate_candidate.

windows.full_history_note: bounded by real OPRA option coverage (option CSVs end ~2026-05-29). 26 trades / 17 bear / 9 bull.

bear_stop_sweep_full_history._note: n=17 bear trades. premstops = # bear trades exiting via EXIT_ALL_PREMIUM_STOP. Demonstrates tight stops whipsaw; the live -10% already sits past the cliff; widening further is flat-to-better.

bull_stop_sweep_full_history._note: n=9 bull trades. BULLISH_RECLAIM is DRAFT per OP-16; the wide cap only prevents catastrophic whipsaw, it does NOT authorize the bull setup. Pattern matches bear: tight stop whipsaws, wide cap rides winners.

edge_capture_no_regression.note: invariant at every stop width -10..-50 — bear stop never touches the J-anchor outcomes (winners ride to TP1/ribbon/time; losers skipped/flat).

anchor_no_regression.note: J winners (4/29,5/01,5/04) captured identically; J loser days 5/05,5/06 not taken (losers_added=$0), 5/07 = +$378 regardless of stop.

oos_pnl_not_materially_worse.note: B2 total +$16,671 vs A +$8,160 — better, not worse, by +$8,511 (+104%).

dsr_advisory.note: B widens the winning margin; -15% is the single strongest (DSR 0.962) but -50% wide-cap is more robust/regime-agnostic and is the blueprint's stated design.

knob_liveness_proof: verified premium_stop_pct_bear is a LIVE knob (not dead): at -2% both bear trades hit the premium stop (bear_pnl -$71); at -10%+ zero bear trades hit it (bear_pnl +$1,188). Rules out the BS-sim-ignored-strike-offset incident class (OP-16 sim-accuracy gate).
why_-50_not_-15: the empirical peak is -15% (total $11,016) but that is a narrow optimum on n=17 bear trades and risks overfitting; a wide -50% cap is the blueprint's explicit 'catastrophe cap' design — regime-agnostic, within 4% of peak, and lets the chart/ribbon/profit-lock be primary. J may tighten to -15..-20% later; documented here.

aggressive_account_NO_SHIP.decision: NO_SHIP on aggressive — chart-stop-primary REGRESSES the Bold account. Per-account verdict (C29/L149: exit knobs don't transfer across accounts/strikes).

agg_bear_stop_sweep._note: total_pnl by bear stop. Current -7% is a sharp local optimum; widening is catastrophic at -20..-35% and only recovers to $365 at -50% vs $7,970 at -7%. Jagged surface, opposite of Safe's smooth plateau.

aggressive_account_NO_SHIP.why_different: Bold uses ITM-2 strikes (higher delta, premium moves more $/contract), 5.0x runner target, looser entry gates (min_triggers=1, require_bearish_fill_bar) -> n=206 trades. With ITM + high frequency the tight premium stop is load-bearing: it cuts many small losers before they run. Widening converts small stop-losses into large ones (WR rises but total P&L collapses). Safe (OTM, selective, n=26) has the opposite economics.
aggressive_account_NO_SHIP.action: aggressive/params.json + aggressive/heartbeat.md LEFT UNCHANGED. Bold keeps bear -7% / bull -5%.

revert_path: SAFE ONLY changed. params.json: restore premium_stop_pct -0.08, premium_stop_pct_bear -0.10, premium_stop_multiplier 0.92; restore heartbeat.md exit-hierarchy text (premium-stop-primary) + filters.py PREMIUM_STOP_PCT_BEAR_DEFAULT. Backups: heartbeat-v14-prod-backup.md (further). aggressive/* was never changed.
